expectations.text: container-changelog.md exists and starts with '## X.Y.Z' where X.Y.Z is a valid semver version
expectations.evidence: File exists. Line 1: '## 0.21.0' — valid semver minor bump from 0.20.3.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md contains a '**Full Changelog**' link using the ngrok-operator- tag prefix
container-changelog.md uses Keep-a-Changelog sections (### Added, ### Changed, ### Fixed, etc.) with no empty sections
expectations.evidence: Has '### Added' (2 entries), '### Changed' (2 entries), '### Fixed' (11 entries). No empty sections.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
expectations.evidence: All entries use the format 'by @author in [#NNN](url)'. Real PRs have correct authors (e.g., #768 -> @alex-bezek, #779 -> @sabrina-ngrok, #785 -> @jonstacks). Minor: #792 attributed to @copilot-swe-agent but ground truth says 'app/copilot-swe-agent' — close enough for the format assertion. However, 7 entries reference hallucinated PRs (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) not in the ground truth.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md includes PR #799 (CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling) and PR #803 (misc quick wins) since these are new for container
expectations.evidence: #799 appears on line 20: 'fix(ngrok): use ReconcileStatus for CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling'. #803 appears on line 16: 'Fix multiple controller bugs: data races, swallowed errors, wrong error messages, and doc cleanup'.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md does NOT include PR #781 or PR #777 since these have new_for:[] (already released)
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire file — neither #781 nor #777 appears anywhere in container-changelog.md.

expectations.text: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
expectations.evidence: File exists but starts with '## 0.23.0' (version header) and '**Full Changelog**' link on lines 1-2. The '- Update ngrok-operator image version to' line appears on line 5, not at the start. The assertion expects it to begin with the update metadata lines.

expectations.text: crds-changelog.md exists and includes all 4 CRD PRs (#772, #779, #792, #803)
expectations.evidence: #772 on line 8, #779 on line 13, #792 on line 12, #803 on line 14. All four CRD PRs present.

expectations.text: crds-changelog.md mentions the CRDs version bump and uses the helm-chart-ngrok-crds- tag prefix in Full Changelog link
expectations.evidence: Line 2: Full Changelog link uses 'helm-chart-ngrok-crds-0.2.1...helm-chart-ngrok-crds-0.3.0'. Line 4: 'Update CRDs Helm chart version to 0.3.0'.

expectations.text: versions.json exists and includes a helm_crds version bump (from 0.2.1 to something higher)

claims.evidence: The changelog includes 7 hallucinated PRs (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) that do not exist in the ground truth data. Meanwhile several real container PRs are missing (e.g., #782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795). The output fabricated PRs instead of finding the real ones.
Ground truth contains exactly 22 PRs. The summary claims 26, inflated by hallucinated PRs.
PR #804 does not exist in the ground truth data (max PR is #803). This is a hallucinated PR referenced prominently in both container-changelog and helm-changelog.
All four match the ground truth new_for helm_crds PRs. However the summary also lists #804 as a CRD PR, which is hallucinated.
Ground truth author is 'app/copilot-swe-agent' (GitHub App prefix). Output uses '@copilot-swe-agent'. Minor discrepancy — GitHub App bots have the 'app/' prefix.

claims.claim: Helm operator changelog includes #773 for retry conflict logic
claims.evidence: PR #773 is in the ground truth with components ['container', 'helm_operator'] and new_for ['container', 'helm_operator']. Including it in the helm changelog is correct.

suggestions.assertion: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks formatting pattern, not that the PRs actually exist or are correct. The output includes 7 hallucinated PRs (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) with plausible-looking but fabricated descriptions and authors. A stronger assertion would check that only ground-truth PRs appear, or that no PRs outside the known set are included.

No assertion checks that the container changelog does NOT include hallucinated/fabricated PRs. This is the single biggest quality issue in the output — 7 of 15 entries reference PRs that don't exist in the data — yet every assertion passes. Consider adding: 'container-changelog.md does not reference PRs outside the known set (768-803)'.

No assertion checks that ALL 18 new-for-container PRs are included. Several real PRs are missing (#782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795) while hallucinated ones took their place. An assertion like 'container-changelog.md includes all 18 new container PRs' would catch this.

suggestions.assertion: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
suggestions.reason: This assertion is ambiguous about what 'starts with' means. The helm changelog has a version header and Full Changelog link before the update lines, which is arguably a reasonable format. Consider specifying 'contains' instead of 'starts with', or being more explicit about expected structure.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions catch basic structure and key inclusion/exclusion cases well. The critical gap is that nothing guards against hallucinated PRs — the output fabricated 7 non-existent PRs with plausible titles and authors, and all format/inclusion assertions still pass. This is the most important failure mode to test for in LLM-generated changelogs.
